author: @MG.50
text:
Interesting to build a Tesla turbine just to pull a vacuum on the flask to cause the water to boil. Hmmm...
    Intriguing project and good shop work. I think I would be more inclined to purchase a vacuum pump (I was looking at some a few weeks ago on the surplus market at very reasonable prices), unless part of the challenge was to make a project using a Tesla turbine.
    I assume the point was to distill water, since making a desalination system was mentioned. I really like the use of a vacuum to boil the water rather than heat. Pressure modification could certainly have advantages if you were working with heat sensitive chemicals. Thank you for providing several thought provoking directions to pursue!
    OH, I almost forgot to ask. How did the discs hold up in the turbine over time? It sounded as if you were limiting the running RPM, which would get past the notorious "discs flying to pieces" issue associated with the Teala turbines, but I wondered about long duration stress. Thanks.

author: @aarondingus3548
text:
Love this idea! It's essentially using outside energy to create a cold steam pump which is awesome.
I think it might be possible to use this as a desalination device and a generator concurrently. If a heat differental was setup between the input and output, then a Tesla pump was added to the same shaft of the turbine, you would essentially have a heat engine generator and desalinatior. 
I may have bungled the explanation, but I'm basically combining the main ideas presented in this video with some newer videos from the channel IEnergySupply.
Anyways, great video as always! I'm truly amazed at the value brought forth by this channel and the great minds that run it

author: @RoadRageLive
text: Boiling the water in a vacuum for desalination, awesome concept.  Instead of plastic, I am going to use old computer equipment .  I like using the aluminum platters from old desktop hard drives for projects like this and I have built a tesla turbine with them.  I even used the spacers and electric motors in the drives to make the tesla turbine into a generator. My issue has always been building the case that makes up the body of the unit, I never thought of laminating plastic but it makes sense.  In the past I have tried bamboo from bamboo flooring, 4 inch drain pipe cut compressed into a small diameter, metal pipes of different diameter but laminating plastic never came to mind.  Thank you!

As ever, very interesting and informative.
A little tip to help you locate your chuck key; place a small magnet on the casing of the pillar drill and use it to park the chuck key. Then the chuck key is always to hand.
Hope that is useful to you. Cheers.

author: @KimberlyRPeacock
text: As an example here is a high speed electrostatic motor. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vlL3XtzYWV0  You could use graphene to enhance.  Place the motor and coupling from motor to tesla turbine in a vacuum.  Use the Kelvin water drop instead as the electrostatic generator.  One can go back to the old scientific american amatuer scientists articles and find a wealth of information.  They used to have a cd with a collection of all the articles ever published. I have one somewhere. If I can find it, I will grab a bunch of articles for you all.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i love the old scientific America - i have one of the books somewhere if i can find it

replies.author: @KimberlyRPeacock
replies.text: Yes, you could use the tesla turbine as an electrostatic generator, and gas flow, or some charged fluid to drive it, and collect and store in a capacitor. High Voltage low current and then transform it.      However why use a tesla turbine? What is the advantage over say a flat sheet of graphene in which the fluid travels over and charges?

replies.author: @KimberlyRPeacock
replies.text:
Leonard Janus Electroactive polymers are variable capacitors.  They can be driven by high voltage and low current as actuators, or they can be generators when deformed.  Check out SRI.  3M VHB 4910 Tape and google electro active polymers, you should find plenty of information on which to build.   At the right temperature and with the right refrigerant, you could build a type of engine that used small temperature difference to convert liquid refrigerant to gas and collect the charge during expansion, and then drive it through the tesla turbine which would generate a charge and then use the polymer as a muscle to compress it again.  During expansion the refrigerant would be cold (endothermic) pulling in energy from ambient, and during compression it would get warm returning heat to ambient.

You are not going to get over unity!

However work is not energy.  Temperature is not energy, they are configurations of energy. Work is the pressure volume change.  which corresponds with the temperature. 

The trick is in storing most of the energy and using it to reverse cycle. Your waveform would be stepwise.  You are trying to create an oscillator, and have it so constrained that it cannot reach equilibrium. Therefore the refrigerant must be at a temperature and pressure that its ready to vaporize. What you want to do in this case is use entropy in your favor.  

For instance say I had a balloon (diaphragm) made of the electroactive polymer, and I coated it such that it would not leak hydrogen, or do so so slowly, that we can ignore it.  What if at one end of this balloon / diaphragm I have an inlet and its connected to a reversible metal hydride, and at the other end I have a drive rod that connects it to another balloon/diaphragm. The reversible metal hydride has an equilibrium pressure of 10 bar at 15 C and it has an endothermic reaction of 19 KJ per mol of hydrogen.  Well as soon as the hydride starts releasing hydrogen into that balloon its temp will start to drop and with it its equilibrium pressure unless we put in heat at 15C from the ambient at the same rate we are releasing the hydrogen.  On the other side we have the same hydride but its temperature is at 0C and its equilibrium pressure is half at 5 Bar, so we have 1/2 the fluid power of the mass flow of hydrogen we can use to power something useful, like driving the tesla turbine.

The charges built up from the turbine driving some other fluid can be used to drive a heat pump but that heat pump say its using propane will have a cop of 16. And yes some of our 50% of power will go to running that subsystem.  However it will run for as long as the temperature is maintained at 15C.

author: @KimberlyRPeacock
text: Excellent work.  It's amazing what you can do with just a few tools and some creativity.  Virtually everyone can afford a vacuum pump.   I would use a piston pump, diaphragm, or aspirator pump as the roughing pump, then use the Tesla as the roots blower.  A little edwards vacuum pump system, which you don't have to give up a months pay for.  The lathe trick is like on a wood lathe, where you could attach the razor to a wooden handle and use a support, or one could mount it to a grinder motor and do the same thing.  This looks like such fun.  I might be tempted to mount an electrostatic motor to the Tesla turbine to drive it, and use a lord kevin's thunderbolt, from the condensing water vapor to drive it.  Electrostatic motors are ideal for this type of application whereby you want high speed but do not need high torque.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering

replies.author: @KimberlyRPeacock
replies.text: I am thinking that you are going to be a happy camper when you do this and add graphene to the mix.  That is a hint.  I leave you the pleasure of discovery.   :)  If you want a better hint just nudge me.  You are going to have fun.
